topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The delivery driver, a young man with an overly bright smile, handed me a bouquet of deep crimson roses. They were impossibly perfect, the petals velvety and dew-kissed, but there was no note. Just the flowers, wrapped in simple, crisp paper. My hands trembled as I took them. Where was the message? Who would send something so extravagant without even a courtesy note? I stared at the arrangement, my breath catching in my throat. A cold knot tightened in my stomach. I quickly tucked the stems into my coat pocket, my eyes darting around the quiet hallway as if expecting someone to materialize from the shadows. Every rustle of fabric sounded like a footstep. This felt wrong. Deeply, fundamentally wrong.

[story 2]

When the doorbell chimed, I hesitated, my heart beginning a frantic, uneven rhythm against my ribs. Standing on the porch was a woman holding a vase overflowing with lilies—pure white, majestic, and utterly silent. She offered a small, tight smile and then retreated before I could even manage a greeting. The silence that followed was deafening. I looked down at the vase, then back at the empty space where she had stood. My palms grew slick. I shuffled toward the entryway, my movements jerky, trying to process the sheer audacity of the gesture. A frantic need to know the context overwhelmed me. I reached out, my fingers brushing the cool, smooth surface of a lily, needing any anchor in this sudden, bewildering situation.

[story 3]

The box landed on my doorstep with a soft thud. Inside, nestled in crinkled tissue paper, were vibrant sunflowers, their faces turned toward the sun. No inscription. Just the blooms. I snatched the package open, my fingers fumbling with the wrapping. My chest felt suddenly constricted, as if someone had clamped a hand over my lungs. Why? Who thought this was appropriate? I stood rigid by the front door, the flowers suddenly feeling heavy, almost burdensome. I paced three steps back and forth across the linoleum floor, my gaze sweeping the empty street, searching for a clue, a sign, anything to explain this inexplicable outpouring of affection. My jaw was clenched so tightly I could feel the strain in my temples.
